title: rubus hirtus var. glandulifer
intro: Rubus hirtus var. glandulifer, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or glandular raspberry, is a variety of wild raspberry plant native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Rubus hirtus var. glandulifer include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and roots can also be used to make a decoction, which is a popular way to utilize its medicinal properties.
